mentions.note: Longtime show favorite, up 239% in 2025 on AI memory demand; still liked, but Cramer wants holders to take some off the table.
Jumping on the same memory/data center shortage dynamic driving the storage group higher.
Memory chip maker up 18% YTD; benefiting from data center demand for storage chips in short supply.
Announced $100B New York fab for memory chip production; critical shortage persists; stock up 27% YTD; data-center demand outpaced supply and will continue to grow.
Memory maker up huge; printing money but in year 2 of shortage; first sniff of supply catch-up will crash this; take profits significantly now.
Memory/storage leader, strong 2025/2026 on genuine shortage; supply will eventually catch up, first sniff of waning demand crashes these stocks—take significant profits.

Memory chip prices up 400–500% on AI demand explosion; parabolic 111%+ annual move unstoppable but historically risky; breaking ground $100B fab (2030 production); buy on pullback, don't chase parabolic.
Up parabolic on 400-500% memory price spike driven by AI demand; breaking ground on $100B fab (2030 production); no peak in sight but historically cycle-prone.
Storage play up 39% YTD; memory shortage supporting extended rally; devices like gasoline—no price resistance due to AI data center demand; Cramer sees further upside even at current levels.
Memory/storage play; endless buying in last year due to data center shortage surge. Shortage-driven rally continues.
Memory chip maker in short supply; data center demand driving shortages.

CEO Sanjay Mehrotra announced $100B foundry in New York (2030), new fab Idaho (H2 2027), new fab Singapore (H2 2028); multi-year production expansion guarantees sustained capex orders for equipment makers.
Memory/storage shortage creating absurd pricing power; CEO Sanjay Mehrotra breaking ground on new foundries; shortage lasts years.
Memory storage play up 45% in January; hottest bull market segment on AI-driven data center demand; tripled in 2025 with strong momentum.
Memory chip maker flying too close to sun; avoid being first man on sun (Richard Pryor reference); hard landing.
Memory shortage most severe in Cramer's career; customers rationing product with no demand destruction possible; prices can skyrocket indefinitely on AI data-processing needs.
Memory stock beneficiary of institutional divergence buying; Samsung and SK Hynix strength signals memory super-cycle; Micron likely follows on capex and demand.
Memory semiconductor benefiting from data center buildout as oil falls.
Memory chip shortage supports near-term demand; part of data center semiconductor buying wave.
Memory shortage theme; beneficiary of data-center buildout; buyable on pullback if oil rises.
Huge move post-earnings digesting; company near $500B market cap. Let it churn; wait for pullback below $18 before adding.
Memory stocks crushed on unproven Google memory-killer; parabolic moves do reverse, but pullback justified—cool off further before blessing.
Only company adding capacity at scale but still sold out; Q1 earnings excellent but stock fell 30% mid-March—now rebounding with war talks; true growth story vs. peers.
Memory chip supplier back from dead; hardware renaissance and data center AI capex driving demand.
Memory chip supplier; up 41% since March 30; strong quarter negative catalyst (came in too hot); not above mid-March high; boom-bust watch.
Memory and storage critical for AI infrastructure; shortages persist as demand outpaces industry capacity to build.
Memory stock up 5% today; essential components for data center servers in short supply; part of broad semis rally.
1-year return +597% (vs S&P +29%). Data-center and AI chip demand fueling semiconductor super-cycle; individual stock selection critical.
Memory and semiconductor manufacturer; supply shortage driving prices higher.
Memory chip provider making significant gains from AI and data center demand.
Memory shortage plays justified; not overvalued provided Chinese don't flood markets with cheap DRAM and NAND.

Clear buy at 12x earnings, down 6% on rotation. Cramer waiting for this pullback. Start gingerly, add on 2-3% further declines. Hardware bet vs. expensive Western Digital (46x). CPU shortage tailwind.
Entered $1T market cap club as 10th largest company; high-bandwidth memory chip leader with 200%+ YTD gains; boom appears unrelenting in AI-driven data center buildout.

Exceptional 48-day march to $1T market cap understandable (HBM shortage real, demand sensational) but historically boom-bust memory industry will crash when production capacity added; too risky at current levels.
Memory manufacturer tracking $100+ EPS next year; massively profitable with real earnings unlike dotcom era; reject .com-bubble fears on quality chip plays.
DRAM shortage driving 266% gain YTD; price target raises continue; can't make chips fast enough; part of endless shortage play.
Stock up 300% YTD; must beat and raise significantly beyond consensus to continue higher. Memory shortage severe; insane pricing power but cost passed to consumers via Apple.

Up >300% YTD; blockbuster Q2 (41.5B rev vs 36B est., 2.51 EPS vs ~1.50 est.); 85% gross margins; secular demand from AI across all verticals; $200B US investment; tightness beyond 2027; only US memory manufacturer.
Data center sell-off partly profit-taking and mistaken on Meta news; likes Micron most—8× earnings multiple too low, secular growth story with multi-year locked pricing advantage.
